toolkit_measurements.note: central=0.292 is paper-sourced and left unchanged pending a dedicated relic validation; the MadDM value below does NOT match it and the discrepancy is unresolved. Do not treat 0.292 as toolkit-reproduced.

maddm_3_2_correct_couplings.provenance: harness-climb-a: reran the eval's compiled MadDM relic executable with a param card carrying correct yh1=1 (Block BSMPARAMS), identity SM quark-rotation blocks, and Block PHASES (see maddm/scripts/slha_complete.py). The chi1chi1_hh channel (3.8%) opens only when the Higgs-portal coupling is nonzero, confirming yh1 is active.

eval_run_value_invalid.why: both eval relic runs logged 'parameter mdl_yh1 not found -> 0'; computed with the Yukawa sector zeroed, so 0.166 is not the benchmark value

toolkit_measurements.open_question: 0.0717 (toolkit) vs 0.292 (paper) differ ~4x. Likely a spec/paper convention mismatch, same family as the blind-spot sign/location issue: the h-chi1-chi1 coupling vanishes near theta~+0.79 in this UFO, not the paper's -0.148. Needs a from-scratch spectrum+relic run before central is changed.
